title: Roubaix Capital Opens $8.5 Million York Space Systems Position, Immediately Making YSS its Largest Holding
author: Josh Kohn-Lindquist
description: Roubaix Capital acquired 382,906 shares of York Space Systems (YSS) for approximately $9.29 million, making it the fund's largest holding at 3.96% of assets. The investment reflects confidence in YSS's vertically-integrated satellite business model, strong 52% sales growth in 2025, $500 million backlog, and expected 48% growth in 2026.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong fundamentals including 52% YoY sales growth, $500M backlog vs $4B market cap, expected 48% growth in 2026, path to profitability with positive EBITDA expected, expanding satellite constellation (33 to 140 by 2027), and attractive valuation at $36.87 with 2027 EPS estimates of $0.57. Institutional investment by Roubaix Capital as largest holding signals confidence.
Mentioned as Roubaix Capital's second-largest holding ($7.60M, 3.5% of AUM) but no specific news or analysis provided in the article.
Listed as third-largest holding in Roubaix Capital's portfolio ($7.52M, 3.5% of AUM) but no specific news or analysis provided.

title: CCC et StandardAero signent un contrat d’entretien de cinq ans avec la Marine américaine
author: Na
description: CCC announced a five-year contract with the U.S. Department of Defense for StandardAero to provide maintenance and repair services for 501K engine components at its Winnipeg facility. The contract supports the U.S. Navy's surface fleet operations. StandardAero has delivered over $160 million in services to U.S. military branches over the past two decades.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: StandardAero secured a significant five-year DoD contract worth over $80 million (referenced in related links) for critical engine maintenance services. This demonstrates continued confidence from the U.S. military, builds on 20 years of successful service delivery totaling $160+ million, and represents substantial recurring revenue for the company.

CCC successfully facilitated another major government-to-government contract between a Canadian company and the U.S. DoD, reinforcing its role as the designated contracting authority and demonstrating its value in enabling Canadian businesses to access U.S. defense procurement opportunities.

title: CCC, StandardAero sign 5-year maintenance contract with U.S. Navy
author: Na
description: CCC has been awarded a new 5-year contract with the U.S. Department of Defense for StandardAero to repair and modify 501K engine components at its Winnipeg facility. These engines are critical power sources for the U.S. Navy's surface fleet. StandardAero has delivered over $160 million in MRO services to U.S. military branches over the past twenty years.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Awarded a significant 5-year U.S. DoD contract for engine maintenance services, demonstrating continued confidence in the company's capabilities and representing substantial revenue opportunity. The contract reinforces StandardAero's established track record of delivering over $160 million in military MRO services.

Successfully facilitated a major defense contract between a Canadian company and the U.S. DoD, demonstrating CCC's role as the designated contracting authority and its ability to enable Canadian businesses to secure significant government contracts.

title: Predictable Power: StandardAero Selected by Robinson Helicopter Company as Preferred MRO Provider for R66 Rolls-Royce RR300 Engines
author: Na
description: StandardAero has been selected as the preferred maintenance, repair, and overhaul (MRO) provider for Robinson Helicopter Company's R66 helicopters powered by Rolls-Royce RR300 engines. The agreement provides predictable turnaround times and reduced costs for operators, with services localized across four StandardAero hubs in North America and the United Kingdom. StandardAero currently supports 150 approved component repairs on the RR300 and is developing an additional 180 repairs.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: StandardAero secured a significant partnership with a major helicopter OEM, expanding its MRO service offerings and securing immediate shop capacity. The agreement demonstrates business growth, increased demand for their services, and positions them as a preferred provider in the rotorcraft aftermarket segment.

Rolls-Royce is mentioned as the engine manufacturer (RR300) but the agreement is between StandardAero and Robinson. While the partnership supports the RR300 ecosystem, there is no direct impact or strategic development mentioned for Rolls-Royce itself.

title: StandardAero Ups Guidance on Earnings
author: Na
description: StandardAero reported strong Q2 2025 financial performance with 13.5% revenue growth, raised full-year guidance, and significant expansion in LEAP engine maintenance bookings and Component Repair Services margins.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company demonstrated robust financial performance with 13.5% revenue growth, increased LEAP bookings to $1.5 billion, 50% adjusted EBITDA growth in Component Repair Services, and raised full-year guidance across revenue and profitability metrics
